desc: Taipei is one of Asia's easiest big cities to use well: a food-heavy, temple-threaded, mountain-edged capital where the MRT makes neighbourhood-hopping simple and where hot springs, hikes, old streets and polished shopping districts all sit in the same compact urban basin. It suits travellers who like cities that feel dense but highly manageable rather than intimidating.

todo.name: Raohe or Ningxia night market
todo.desc: Build an evening around small dishes, queues and repeat laps rather than one sit-down meal.

todo.name: Taipei 101 and Xiangshan
todo.desc: Pair the tower district with Elephant Mountain for the best skyline perspective.

todo.name: Beitou hot springs
todo.desc: Ride the MRT to hot springs and Japanese-era architecture when you want a slower half-day.

todo.name: Longshan Temple and old streets
todo.desc: Use Wanhua for temple life, herbal alleys and historic Taipei texture.

todo.name: Yongkang, Zhongshan or Ximending district drift
todo.desc: Give yourself time to explore one district without a checklist, because Taipei's appeal often comes from cafe, design and snack-shop rhythm rather than single monuments.

todo.name: Geothermal Valley with Beitou stop
todo.desc: Pair the Beitou area with the steaming valley and museum zone rather than treating it as just a quick soak detour.

wifi.note: Excellent across most hotels, cafes, stations and commercial districts, making Taipei one of the easier cities here for fully online navigation and trip changes.

prac.fltNote: Taipei's long-haul reality runs through Taoyuan rather than Songshan, and the city is easy to reach from the airport thanks to the Airport MRT and in-town check-in options. For UK travellers, the important distinction is that Taiwan has London Heathrow service rather than LGW or LCY, so destination-level direct flags remain false only because the renderer tracks Gatwick and City. Songshan is useful mostly for regional and domestic legs once you are already in Taiwan.
prac.lang: Mandarin Chinese; English support varies by district and transport hub
prac.visa: British citizens can visit Taiwan visa-free for up to 90 days, and GOV.UK says your passport must be valid for at least 6 months after arrival. You must also complete the Taiwan Arrival Card online within 3 days before arrival.
alerts: Typhoon season can disrupt flights and outdoor plans.
Summer humidity is intense; plan indoor breaks.
Popular night-market stalls can involve queues even on weeknights.
Do not confuse Taoyuan and Songshan when booking flights, especially if you are connecting onward in Asia.

sweet: October to December and April are usually Taipei's best balance of manageable humidity, strong food-and-walking energy and fewer weather headaches than peak summer.
